title: cestrum jacaltenanginum var. tomentosum
intro: Cestrum jacaltenanginum var. tomentosum, also known as the tomentose night-blooming jasmine or Mexican stinkweed, is a flowering plant species native to Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antispasmodic and anticonvulsant effects, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids. The health benefits of Cestrum jacaltenanginum var. tomentosum include its potential to reduce anxiety and stress, improve sleep quality, and alleviate symptoms of epilepsy and other neurological disorders. The plant can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
